title: breynia gour-maitii
intro: Breynia gour-maitii, also known as Nal-tam or Devil's Backbone, is a tropical plant native to India and Southeast Asia, characterized by its thorny stems and red berries. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for centuries, with medicinal properties attributed to its leaves and roots, which contain flavonoids, glycosides, and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Breynia gour-maitii include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, which are said to help treat various ailments such as fever, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and roots are often dried, powdered, and used in teas, decoctions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties.
